appeal_letter:
Dear Cigna Appeals Reviewer,

I am appealing the denial of coverage for the MRI of the Cervical Spine performed on 04/22/2026.

The denial states that coverage is denied because prior authorization was not obtained before the procedure was performed. The letter explains that, under the plan documents, advanced imaging such as MRI, CT, and PET requires prior authorization from Cigna to confirm the service is medically appropriate, and that because my provider did not submit a prior authorization request prior to the date of service, the claim was denied and I was held responsible for the full cost.

I ask the reviewer to apply that prior-authorization requirement to the specific facts of this case. The MRI was not an elective or routine scan that could be scheduled in advance. Following a minor car accident, I experienced sudden, severe numbness and weakness in my left arm. The emergency room physician ordered the MRI immediately to rule out a spinal cord injury. In that emergency setting there was no opportunity to wait for the standard prior-authorization window before the imaging was performed. The purpose the plan assigns to prior authorization, confirming that the service is medically appropriate, was satisfied here by the urgent clinical circumstances: the imaging was ordered by an emergency physician to evaluate an acute neurological deficit and exclude a spinal cord injury. Denying coverage solely because authorization could not be obtained in advance of an emergent, physician-ordered scan does not reflect the documented clinical facts, and I ask that the individualized facts of this emergency warrant an exception to the advance-authorization requirement.

I also note that the denial letter does not state the deadline for filing this appeal, the process for submitting it, or my right to a full and fair review of the decision. I assert my right to a complete and fair review of this denial, and I request that you provide the applicable appeal deadline and procedures in your response.

I request that Cigna overturn the denial, conduct a full and fair review, and approve coverage for the cervical spine MRI performed on 04/22/2026 in light of the emergency circumstances under which it was ordered.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Respectfully,
Member
missing_evidence_checklist: ER physician's order and notes documenting the acute left-arm numbness and weakness and the indication to rule out spinal cord injury
Emergency department records and timeline showing the MRI was performed during the emergency visit
Any plan-document language addressing prior authorization for emergency or urgent advanced imaging
The MRI report and any findings
Documentation of the car accident and its date relative to the 04/22/2026 service
The applicable appeal deadline and filing instructions from Cigna
